assessment.title: Session 10 Quiz – Advanced Array and Object Techniques

material.mattext:
Session 10 Quiz – Advanced Array and Object Techniques

Test your understanding of advanced array methods, object manipulation, and data transformation.

Topics Covered: - Chaining array methods - Nested array and object access - Object.assign and spread for objects - Array sorting and comparison - Data transformation patterns - Working with complex data structures

Instructions: - 16 questions from the Session 10 question bank - 20 minute time limit - 3 attempts allowed - 16 points total (1 point per question)

After syncing the question bank to Canvas:
A new array containing only elements where the callback returns true
The index of the first match
What does this produce? ```js const nums = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]; const evens = nums.filter(n => n % 2 === 0); console.log(evens); ```
The first element matching a condition
A new array where each element is the result of the callback
What does this produce? ```js const prices = [10, 20, 30]; const withTax = prices.map(p => p * 1.1); console.log(withTax[0]); ```
.find() returns the first matching element; .filter() returns all matches
What does `.findIndex()` return when no match is found?
What does this produce? ```js const users = [{id: 1, name: "Alex"}, {id: 2, name: "Jordan"}]; const user = users.find(u => u.id === 2); console.log(user.name); ```
What is wrong with sorting numbers without a comparator? ```js [10, 9, 2, 100].sort() ```
Sorts as strings: [10, 100, 2, 9] — wrong numeric order
Which comparator sorts numbers in ascending order?
A shallow copy of the array
A reference to the same array
Why spread before sorting? ```js const sorted = [...original].sort((a, b) => a - b); ```
.sort() mutates in place; spread preserves the original
.sort() requires an array copy to work

What does this chain produce? ```js const students = [ {name: "Alex", grade: 85}, {name: "Jordan", grade: 55}, {name: "Casey", grade: 92} ]; const result = students .filter(s => s.grade >= 70) .map(s => s.name); console.log(result); ```
What does the spread operator do when applied to objects? ```js const base = {name: "Alex", score: 85}; const updated = {...base, score: 92}; ```
Creates a new object with all base properties, overriding score with 92
A new array of the first 3 elements
Calling the same method multiple times
Calling methods in sequence where each returns an array for the next to act on
Using a loop inside a method
A special syntax for arrays only
What does this return? ```js const items = ["b", "a", "c"]; const sorted = [...items].sort(); console.log(items[0]); ```
A JavaScript runtime for running JS outside the browser
Node Package Manager for installing packages
How do you run a JavaScript file with Node.js?
Which command initializes a new Node.js project?
How do you install a package with npm?
How do you export a function named `add` using ES6 module syntax?
`export function add(a, b) { return a + b; }`
How do you import the `add` function from `./math.js` using ES6 syntax?
How do you enable ES6 modules in Node.js?
What's the difference between Node.js and browser JavaScript?
Node.js runs on server/computer, browser JS runs in browser
Where are npm packages stored after installation?
What can Node.js access that browser JavaScript cannot?
What does this do? ```js import chalk from 'chalk'; console.log(chalk.blue('Hello')); ```
Prints "Hello" in blue color in terminal
Creates (or overwrites) log.txt with the text "hello"
What second argument is required to get a string back from `fs.readFileSync`?
What does this print when run with `node calc.js multiply 4 5`? ```js const a = Number(process.argv[3]); const b = Number(process.argv[4]); console.log(a * b); ```
Why must you wrap `process.argv` values with `Number()` before doing arithmetic?
What path prefix is required when importing your own local files?
